title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction · Cost-Model Clinic: Dp Recurrence
summary: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 dp recurrence increment in a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
